title: Your Project Title
shortDescription: A concise summary of what students will build (10-200 characters).
description: A detailed description of the project вЂ” what it does, what students will learn, why it's valuable for their portfolio, and what real-world skills it covers.

features.title: User Authentication
features.description: Secure login and registration with JWT tokens, password hashing, and session management.

features.title: CRUD Operations
features.description: Create, read, update, and delete resources with proper validation and error handling.

features.title: Responsive Dashboard
features.description: A clean, mobile-friendly dashboard that displays user data with charts and filters.

features.title: Search & Filtering
features.description: Full-text search with category filters and pagination for browsing content.

milestones.title: Project Setup & Planning
milestones.description: Initialize the project, install dependencies, set up the folder structure, and plan the database schema.
tasks: Run create-react-app or Vite for frontend

milestones.title: Backend API Development
milestones.description: Build the REST API with all routes, controllers, middleware, and database models.
tasks: Create User model with password hashing
Test all endpoints with Postman/Thunder Client

milestones.title: Frontend UI Development
milestones.description: Build the React frontend with all pages, components, and state management.
tasks: Create dashboard page with data fetching
Build CRUD forms and list views

milestones.title: Polish & Deployment
milestones.description: Add finishing touches, write tests, optimize performance, and deploy to production.
tasks: Set up environment variables for production

architecture:
Client-Server architecture:
- Frontend: React SPA with React Router for navigation
- Backend: Express.js REST API with MVC pattern
- Database: MongoDB with Mongoose ODM
- Auth: JWT-based with refresh tokens
- Communication: Axios for HTTP requests

databaseConsiderations: Use MongoDB with Mongoose ODM. Design schemas with proper indexing for query performance. Use references (ObjectId) for relationships between collections. Add timestamps to all documents. Consider using MongoDB Atlas free tier (512MB) for development and production.
apiConsiderations: RESTful API design with consistent response format: { success: boolean, data: any, message: string }. Use HTTP status codes properly (200, 201, 400, 401, 404, 500). Implement rate limiting for auth routes. Use helmet.js for security headers. Add CORS configuration for frontend domain.
testingGuidance: Start with unit tests for utility functions and API route handlers using Jest. Add integration tests for the auth flow (register в†’ login в†’ access protected route). Use React Testing Library for component tests. Aim for at least 60% coverage on critical paths.
securityConsiderations: Hash passwords with bcrypt (12 rounds). Store JWT secret in environment variables, never in code. Sanitize all user inputs to prevent XSS and injection. Use helmet.js for HTTP security headers. Implement rate limiting on authentication endpoints. Validate request bodies with Joi or Zod.
deploymentGuidance: Frontend: Deploy to Vercel (connect GitHub repo, auto-deploys on push). Backend: Deploy to Railway or Render (free tier available). Database: Use MongoDB Atlas free tier. Set environment variables in each platform's dashboard. Use a custom domain if available.

ideas: Build an admin dashboard with analytics
Add CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions
Add monitoring with Sentry error tracking

seo.title: Build a Full-Stack App вЂ” Guided Project
seo.description: Step-by-step guided project to build a production-ready full-stack application with React, Node.js, and MongoDB.
